title: Asset
description: Bounded contract for every file under agents/assets/**. An asset must declare WHAT it is (name + description) and WHO uses it (current_consumers). The body shape varies by asset category (definitions, guidelines, examples, templates) so additional properties are permitted — but the contract is enforced. An asset with zero current_consumers is an orphan and must be deleted or adopted per guidance-writer's escalation rules.

name.description: Slug matching the asset's filename without extension (e.g. `tools-base` for `tools-base.yml`).

description.description: One-paragraph plain-English statement of what this asset is and when an agent should reference it. Not a summary of the body; a framing of it.

current_consumers.description: Paths (repo-root-relative) to the consumers of this asset. Two consumer shapes are valid: agent spec triplet files (the original consumer class) and story files (added per ADR-0007 decision 2 so stories can also reference cross-corpus concepts). At least one entry required. Reciprocity with each story consumer's assets: field is a corpus-level invariant per ADR-0007 decision 4; guidance-writer / story-writer audits check that every listed consumer genuinely references this asset's path.
